content:
THE AI DEBATE
It’s not “AI vs human writers.” It’s about which humans learn to use it well, and which don’t.

4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Zach M on UnsplashDisclaimer: These are my personal opinions, formed from my own experience writing and using AI tools, not universal facts. I’d genuinely love to hear where you agree or disagree.
Every week there’s a new argument online. One side says AI is going to replace writers. The other side says real writers would never touch it. I personally think both sides are arguing about the wrong thing.
The Fear Isn’t Really About AI
When writers say they’re afraid of AI, I don’t think the fear is really about the tool. It’s about being replaced by something cheaper and faster, without anyone checking whether “faster” also means “worse.” That fear is fair. A lot of AI-generated content online right now is bland, repetitive, and clearly written by nobody in particular. People can feel that, even when they can’t explain why.
But here’s what I don’t think gets said enough: the problem was never the tool; it’s that the tool doesn’t have a point of view, and a lot of the content flooding the internet right now is…
